page: Microsoft Azure D8as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv5 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 8 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include any local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% at 2702.98. Multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench scores fall within the average range. Memory read bandwidth is average, reaching 512.91 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 41.19 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. However, large block memory write bandwidth is relatively weak at 24.52 GB/sec, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also registers in the bottom 25%. Static web serving throughput averages 10.04 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations sustain 8.2 million operations per second.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D8as_v5 offers standard resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads. The absence of bundled local storage requires reliance on external Premium Storage, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. This virtual machine is well-suited for web serving, database operations, software compilation, and light text or image processing tasks that do not require GPU acceleration or high-speed local NVMe drives.
description: Microsoft Azure D8as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv5 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 x86_64 processor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, a 3.525 GHz clock speed, and 32.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks bundled local storage and GPUs, but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core execution and memory write bandwidth are average to weak. This configuration is suitable for general-purpose workloads, web serving, database operations, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8as_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and Premium Storage capability, delivering strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8as_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance, suitable for web serving and databases.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and strong single-core performance for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB RAM
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Premium Storage capable virtual machine with no bundled local storage
Optimized for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and database operations
